name: appflowy-api
description: AppFlowy Cloud/GoTrue API 的认证与调用流程（获取 token、workspace/文档/数据库/搜索等）。在本仓库用 Python 编写或调试 AppFlowy API 客户端、脚本、自动化或排查接口问题时使用。
capabilities: Authenticate with AppFlowy Cloud using GoTrue OAuth via email/password
List and query workspaces, databases, and document views
Create and manipulate document blocks and page views
Perform CRUD operations on database grids and rows
Process Y.js collaborative document state for bulk operations
author: BaloneGit
securitySummary: Evaluated 314 static findings across 42 files. All detected patterns are false positives: external commands are legitimate subprocess calls to trusted local Node.js scripts for Y.js CRDT processing; network access is for AppFlowy API calls to user-controlled endpoints; environment file access is opt-in only via explicit --env flag; filesystem operations are for reading stdin and template files. No evidence of malicious intent, data exfiltration, or unauthorized access. This is a legitimate API client toolkit for self-hosted AppFlowy automation.
valueStatement: Eliminate manual AppFlowy API calls with this Python toolkit. Handle authentication, document operations, database management, and collaborative editing in self-hosted AppFlowy environments.
limitations: Requires self-hosted AppFlowy Cloud instance with API access
API compatibility tied to AppFlowy Cloud version 0.12.3
Y.js collab features require Node.js runtime for subprocess execution
No support for real-time WebSocket operations or live collaboration

useCases.title: Migrate Content to AppFlowy
useCases.description: Transfer existing documentation and structured data from legacy systems to self-hosted AppFlowy. Create workspaces, document hierarchies, and populate database grids programmatically using Python scripts.

useCases.title: Bulk Update Document Metadata
useCases.description: Apply systematic changes across multiple documents and databases. Update field values, add new columns, or restructure data using template-based grid operations without manual editing.

useCases.title: Integrate AppFlowy with CI/CD Pipelines
useCases.description: Automate document generation and updates as part of deployment workflows. Create release notes, update user documentation, or sync database records from external data sources.

promptTemplates.title: Get Authentication Token
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the appflowy-api skill to obtain an access token for my self-hosted AppFlowy instance. My email is user@example.com and password is mypass123. The GoTrue URL is http://localhost:9999.
promptTemplates.scenario: First-time setup to authenticate with AppFlowy Cloud

promptTemplates.title: List All Workspaces
promptTemplates.prompt: Using appflowy-api, list all available workspaces in my AppFlowy Cloud instance. I already have an access token: eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...
promptTemplates.scenario: Discover workspace IDs before performing operations

promptTemplates.title: Create Document from Template
promptTemplates.prompt: Use appflowy-api to create a new document in workspace abc123 using the user management template from references/templates/user_management_doc.json. My token is available in the config.
promptTemplates.scenario: Generate structured documents from predefined templates

promptTemplates.title: Update Database Grid Records
promptTemplates.prompt: With appflowy-api, upsert rows into database def456 in workspace abc123. Use the fitness plan template at references/templates/fitness_plan.example.json. Update existing rows and add new ones based on the template structure.
promptTemplates.scenario: Synchronize external data with AppFlowy database grids

bestPractices: Store AppFlowy URLs and credentials in config files rather than command-line arguments. Use references/config.example.json as a template and avoid hardcoding sensitive values in scripts.
Run the doctor.py script first to verify connectivity and version compatibility before performing bulk operations. This prevents partial updates due to API mismatches.
Test API operations with read-only commands like list-workspaces or search before executing write operations that modify documents or databases.
antiPatterns: Do not pass passwords directly as command-line arguments in production environments. Use --env flag with a .env file or set environment variables for credentials.
Avoid performing database grid updates without backing up existing data. The upsert operations modify records in-place and mistakes can overwrite valuable information.
Never share access tokens or include them in Git repositories. Tokens expire but can be abused before expiration if leaked.

faq.question: Does this skill work with AppFlowy Cloud hosted services?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for self-hosted AppFlowy Cloud instances. It may work with hosted AppFlowy if you have API access, but it is primarily tested against self-hosted deployments.

faq.question: Why does the skill require Node.js for some operations?
faq.answer: Collaborative editing features use Y.js (a CRDT library) to process document state. The Node.js scripts handle complex Y.js operations that are difficult to implement in Python alone.

faq.question: Is my .env file automatically loaded?
faq.answer: No. Environment file loading is opt-in only. You must explicitly pass --env <path> to read variables from a .env file. By default, the skill uses command-line arguments or environment variables.

faq.question: What AppFlowy Cloud version is supported?
faq.answer: The skill is tested against AppFlowy Cloud version 0.12.3. The doctor.py script checks the /api/health endpoint and warns if versions do not match.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes a unified entry point (appflowy_skill.py) that makes it easy to invoke from Claude Code. Run commands like 'python scripts/appflowy_skill.py list' to see available operations.

faq.question: How do I troubleshoot connection errors?
faq.answer: First, run doctor.py with your credentials to diagnose issues. Common problems include firewall rules blocking ports 80/443, incorrect base URLs, or AppFlowy Cloud service not running. Check container connectivity if using Docker.
